[2018-06-18 17:58] leary93: Thanks to that threat

[2018-06-18 18:00] cactus_wren (cactus): i'm just glad i played both MTG and chess before trying prismata
[2018-06-18 18:00] cactus_wren (cactus): it has tons of elements of both

[2018-06-18 19:08] cogito3 (Cogito): @Sakuya: Depends on the situation, generally I'd say yes, note though that Odin is at its best when you're not clicking it, so it's not as good if you have a bigger absorber (Xaetron excepted since you can alternate which you absorb on)
[2018-06-18 19:16] shadourow (Rando): I think that In base + Odin Matrix, it's better to click odin every turn if you're already above absorb without Odin, as you're effectively trading a steelspitter against a wall + engi, and that wall supply is important if you don't have other 10+ supply soak
[2018-06-18 19:23] cogito3 (Cogito): in base + odin matrix I'm actually not sure if it's correct to even buy Odin
[2018-06-18 19:27] shadourow (Rando): Eh ? Why ? Odin is viable in Base + odin, how matrix would change that ? Matrix makes blue even easier to spend !
[2018-06-18 19:29] leary93: Ye pretty sure odin + matrix makes odin even better
[2018-06-18 19:30] leary93: Also, clicking odin does remove splitters, so unless you can replenish your attack while clicking, im not sure odin click is that great in a matrix set
[2018-06-18 19:30] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): dunno, matrix+ odin is kinda meh
[2018-06-18 19:30] flameberger: if you have a matrix and an unclicked Odin, what's the Odin doing?
[2018-06-18 19:31] extratricky: losing odin on defense is not a bad option
[2018-06-18 19:31] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): I mean, you can use the odin as infusion grid soak and it`s 20BBB for infusion+ triple splitter, not that bad
[2018-06-18 19:32] crash_overlord: + your opponent had to buy an aditional 4 defense
[2018-06-18 19:32] crash_overlord: I often use it like that if there's actual infusion or another good absorber in the set
[2018-06-18 19:33] crash_overlord: the question was for matrix
[2018-06-18 19:33] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): you either attack with odin or you sac it, overdefending it for the threat just sucks
[2018-06-18 19:33] leary93: You can use it to overdefend vs opp odin :POGchair:
[2018-06-18 19:33] extratricky: on the bright side you are probably only losing it if they click their odin
[2018-06-18 19:33] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): clicking odin is a good move if you have matrix and kill an odin
[2018-06-18 19:34] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): so you don`t feel bad about doing that
[2018-06-18 19:34] crash_overlord: I don't think it's good
[2018-06-18 19:34] crash_overlord: you still have to buy the odin threat damage cause they could absorb on it
[2018-06-18 19:34] crash_overlord: it's good at some point in the game
[2018-06-18 19:35] extratricky: it's definitely not good in every position, but it's also definitely good in some positions
[2018-06-18 19:35] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): I`d do it at any point in the game
[2018-06-18 19:35] crash_overlord: That's why you're not 2.3k
[2018-06-18 19:35] Deleted User (Deleted User): matrix makes your own odin better but opp's matrix makes your odin worse
[2018-06-18 19:35] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): get 2200 first scrub
[2018-06-18 19:36] crash_overlord: I need to get 2100 before that

--- Thread (48 messages, 2018-06-19) ---
[2018-06-19 00:32] willflame: what units would make that strategy viable? would you want to spam cheap units like Perforators, or buy a couple as attackers and then purchase more efficient ones later like Cynestra in order to force the opponent to pick?
[2018-06-19 00:34] shadourow (Rando): Actually, it depends more of the defensive options, not the offensive ones
[2018-06-19 00:34] namington: well, perfs are mostly soak, and tbh you're happy if the opponent is using apollo on perfs
[2018-06-19 00:35] namington: then they spent 13BBB with BT2 on 2 damage a turn
[2018-06-19 00:35] namington: that's legitimately worse than sniping tarsiers, which is already losing
[2018-06-19 00:35] shadourow (Rando): If there is apollo + great soak, like plexo, or infusion grid and a great absorber, I don't think you can beat Apollo with cheap -3Hp attackers
[2018-06-19 00:35] namington: although that is true
[2018-06-19 00:36] shadourow (Rando): But you will need a wall, then apollo will snipe wall or tarsiers
[2018-06-19 00:36] namington: the dynamic changes if theres really quality soak
[2018-06-19 00:36] namington: like plexo or even, like, polywall
[2018-06-19 00:36] namington: but still im pretty sure sniping perfs is basically always losing
[2018-06-19 00:36] namington: if you do it more than once or twice
[2018-06-19 00:36] shadourow (Rando): Yup, but the question is : why would you ?
[2018-06-19 00:36] namington: if it nets you a breach or saves you from a breach
[2018-06-19 00:36] namington: and uh, thats about it
[2018-06-19 00:36] shadourow (Rando): Sniping animus is way better 😐
[2018-06-19 00:37] namington: sniping animus can be good vs those sort of strats if there's really good soak + absorb in the set
[2018-06-19 00:37] namington: altho TBH you probably wouldve won with any other blue "attacker" in that case
[2018-06-19 00:38] shadourow (Rando): Sniping Animus can be nice against units that consume red or if your opponent want to buy Iceblades
[2018-06-19 00:39] shadourow (Rando): That's still a 6 gold building (but probably worse in most of the cases to kill than wall)
[2018-06-19 00:41] namington: 13BBB BT2 for 6 gold worth of defense a turn isnt particularly efficient
[2018-06-19 00:41] namington: compare to, say, cynestra which kills roughly a wall each turn
[2018-06-19 00:41] namington: that said, it can absolutely work
[2018-06-19 00:42] namington: apollo is kinda weird cause it can be oppressive on sets even if it's poor value
[2018-06-19 00:42] 1m2o: i don't think you ever cynestra
[2018-06-19 00:42] 1m2o: if there are good attacker +3 health you can go for them if it's apolo set
[2018-06-19 00:42] namington: oh yeah never go cynestra vs apple
[2018-06-19 00:42] 1m2o: but if there are not and not good soak you should be able to win with -3 hp attackers
[2018-06-19 00:42] namington: i'm comparing cynestra to apollo here
[2018-06-19 00:42] namington: on the "kills 6 gold per turn" point
[2018-06-19 00:42] namington: obviously if apple is in the set, never commit to cynestra
[2018-06-19 00:43] namington: like I struggle to think of a single set where its correct
[2018-06-19 00:43] namington: maybe if you, uh, somehow shift ampo one turn, then shift cynestra the next can maybe be ok?
[2018-06-19 00:44] namington: you can go for cynestra if your opponent commits to not-apple, but I doubt theres a set where cynestra is the optimal choice when apollo is still viable from your opponent
[2018-06-19 00:44] shadourow (Rando): It seems that apollo isn't base set viable, so yeah, you need soak to support apple
[2018-06-19 00:44] 1m2o: I mean if you pressure him he can not go apoo
[2018-06-19 00:44] amalloy: buying cynestra to sink the 4th red from your apollo seems pretty good. you just need all the drones
[2018-06-19 00:44] 1m2o: like it's fine unless he is going for apolo
[2018-06-19 00:45] namington: yeah if your opponent is forced to commit away from apollo
[2018-06-19 00:45] namington: then buying cyn is fine
[2018-06-19 00:45] namington: but i mean if apollo is still viable in the situation
[2018-06-19 00:45] namington: im pretty sure cynestra is always bad
[2018-06-19 00:45] namington: well, almost always - im sure there's _some_ counterexample
[2018-06-19 00:46] namington: like buy a single cynestra then pivot to tarsiers when your opponent gets forges
[2018-06-19 00:46] namington: if you think tarsiers still beat heavy blue committal in that set
[2018-06-19 00:47] namington: regardless of "wasting" gold for a cynestra that gets sniped in ~4 turns (which, TBH, cynestra still got value in that case)
[2018-06-19 00:47] namington: yeah, maybe im overestimating how good apollo is vs cynestra in hindsight
[2018-06-19 00:47] namington: obviously if your opponetn actively owns apollo don't buy cynestra unless you have multiple other good targets tho
